sourceAuthority: Source documents own semantics; this manifest binds public pages but creates no doctrine.

levels.title: Boundary frames
levels.subtitle: The Titans are selected roles, not arithmetic operands
levels.summary: D0 marks the boundary before positive articulation. The tokens 0, 1, and infinity may play selected symbolic roles, while ordinary 0 and 1 remain numbers and projective infinity belongs to a changed structure.
levels.inherited: Projective geometry and ordinary arithmetic are inherited mathematics [A]; the Titan reading is Emergentist interpretation [I].
levels.boundary: No field theorem says 0 times infinity equals 1. The emblem is not an operation license, substance, agent, or forced generator of reality.

transition.label: Origin aperture
transition.saturation: None is claimed. mu0 names an origin aperture, not exhaustion of a prior positive freedom.
transition.capability: Stable distinction and arithmetic become expressible.
transition.recovery: D1 must preserve the boundary/operand distinction.
transition.prediction: Any adequate D1 account must distinguish ordinary operands from frame roles without using forbidden Titan arithmetic.
transition.alternatives: Arithmetic may be treated as primitive, constructed differently, or grounded without a D0 aperture.
transition.kill: A contradiction-free account that requires Titan tokens to be ordinary operands, or evidence that no origin distinction is needed, defeats this aperture reading.

levels.title: Distinction and arithmetic
levels.subtitle: Numbers operate only inside a declared structure
levels.summary: D1 owns distinction, arithmetic, inversion, limits, and type-correct boundary behavior. Division by zero remains undefined in a field; mapping to a projective pole changes the structure and must be named.
levels.inherited: Field axioms, directional limits, inversion, logarithms, and projective extension are inherited mathematics [A].
levels.boundary: A limit toward infinity is not a field quotient by zero, and projective infinity is not an ordinary real number.

transition.label: Configuration aperture
transition.saturation: One-dimensional values and limits cease to display relations among many inputs, outputs, and charts economically.
transition.capability: Functions, graphs, relations, atlases, and geometry become explicit configurations.
transition.recovery: Every D2 configuration must recover valid D1 values and operations on its declared domain.
transition.evidence: [S/I] formal example; not proof of universal emergence
transition.prediction: A configuration representation should make some D1 limit behavior finite and inspectable without changing the underlying arithmetic result.
transition.alternatives: The added representation may be a convenience, compression, or definitional extension rather than an ontic emergence.
transition.kill: If the proposed D2 representation adds no relational capability or fails to recover its D1 values, this crossing fails.

levels.title: Configuration
levels.subtitle: Functions, relations, graphs, and geometry
levels.summary: D2 owns configuration: inputs and outputs related across a domain, graphs and atlases, and geometric organization. A finite atlas illustrates a family; it is never all mathematical functions at once.
levels.inherited: Function theory, topology, geometry, and coordinate charts are inherited mathematics [A].
levels.boundary: A picture may reveal structure hidden on a line, but visualization alone proves neither a new ontology nor strong emergence.

transition.label: State-assignment aperture
transition.saturation: Classical configuration alone does not encode the full probability assignments associated with incompatible quantum measurement contexts.
transition.capability: Hilbert-space states, density operators, POVMs, and Born probability assignments become available.
transition.recovery: Quantum states must reproduce the declared measurement probabilities and classical limits where those limits apply.
transition.prediction: The D3 representation must distinguish states that agree on one context but differ on another.
transition.alternatives: The lift may be a reducible formal representation rather than a new degree of reality.
transition.kill: If classical configuration suffices for all relevant probability assignments, or the D3 formalism adds no discriminating predictions, the emergence reading fails.

levels.title: Quantum state
levels.subtitle: Probability assignment before a particular actual record
levels.summary: D3 is represented by a density operator rho together with a measurement context. It assigns Born probabilities to possible records. Valid D3 states may carry momentum distributions; uncertainty follows from noncommuting observables, not from an absence of momentum.
levels.inherited: Density operators, POVMs, Born probabilities, Fourier duality, and uncertainty relations are inherited quantum theory [A].
levels.boundary: No realized D4 event does not mean zero change, zero momentum, or a timeless state. This scaffold does not solve measurement, quantum gravity, or quantum interpretation.

transition.label: Actual-record aperture
transition.saturation: A probability assignment, even if exhaustively specified, is not itself a particular performed interaction and recorded outcome.
transition.capability: An actual carrier, interaction, event, and receipted record enter the description.
transition.recovery: Repeated D4 records must remain statistically answerable to the D3 probability model under the stated preparation and measurement conditions.
transition.prediction: Intervening on preparation or measurement context changes the distribution of D4 outcomes as the D3 model specifies.
transition.alternatives: Everettian, Copenhagen-family, hidden-variable, relational, and other interpretations disagree about actualization while sharing operational predictions.
transition.kill: Conflating a probability scalar with a sampled outcome, or failing the model's repeated-run statistics, defeats the proposed bridge.

levels.title: Causal actuality
levels.subtitle: Carriers, interactions, actions, outcomes, and receipts
levels.summary: D4 is the register of what actually occurs: embodied means, attempted action, interaction, outcome, and record. Spacetime, bodies, physical light cones, and cognition are application lenses inside actuality rather than alternate definitions of the rung.
levels.inherited: Physical dynamics and spacetime constraints remain those of the relevant sciences; Emergentism supplies no replacement law.
levels.boundary: A selector can issue a commitment receipt but cannot manufacture the world's outcome receipt. Physical light cones remain bounded by spacetime and c.

transition.label: Counterfactual-capacity aperture
transition.saturation: Actual histories alone do not supply an agent with explicit alternatives, rankings, and conditional plans.
transition.capability: A finite agent models possible futures, ranks options, coordinates, and selects a reachable action.
transition.recovery: Every D5 model token is an actual D4 inscription or process, and every enacted choice returns to D4 consequence.
transition.prediction: Intervening on represented future content, while holding relevant means fixed, changes present action distributions.
transition.alternatives: Behavior may be explained by reactive policy, cached control, or lower-level dynamics without a distinct counterfactual capacity.
transition.kill: If future-content interventions produce no discriminable action change beyond reactive controls, the distinct-capacity claim fails.

levels.title: Modeled possibility and selection
levels.subtitle: Possible content carried by actual model tokens
levels.summary: D5 is the option field an agent can model, rank, coordinate, and attempt to reach. Anticipated futures influence present selection through actual models; this is model-mediated future influence, not physical causation traveling backward in time.
levels.inherited: Decision theory, control, planning, counterfactual reasoning, and institutional coordination supply comparison classes.
levels.boundary: Lowercase phi and nu are reciprocal chart coordinates. Uppercase Phi measures option-field quality or foresight; V is usable D4 means. P_node = Phi times V is a selected model, not a universal law.

transition.label: Exit boundary
transition.saturation: The scaffold has reached its last selected positive register; no universal exhaustion of possibility is claimed.
transition.capability: No new positive freedom. The map exposes its own boundary and the reader's option to leave it.
transition.recovery: D6 must preserve all lower distinctions without pretending to complete them.
transition.prediction: A useful framework should remain usable after its metaphysical imagery is removed and should permit correction or exit.
transition.alternatives: Further positive registers may exist; the selected scaffold may be incomplete or domain-specific.
transition.kill: A genuinely distinct positive freedom beyond D5 requires extending or replacing the census rather than defending closure.

levels.title: Exit and nonclosure
levels.subtitle: The map marks its limit and cannot certify itself
levels.summary: D6 opens no positive freedom. It marks the point where the description stops, its unresolved questions remain visible, and the reader may put the map down.
levels.inherited: Formal limits motivate humility but do not prove this scaffold complete.
levels.boundary: There is no additional μ-crossing after μ4, and D6 is not identical to D0. The return edge r6 means boundary-role resemblance only, not recurrence, identity, topology, or self-similarity.

return.label: Interpretive return
return.meaning: D6 points back to D0 because both are selected limit markers. The relation preserves difference and adds no theorem.
